description_long: This is a streamlined, bright orange military jet airplane with a smooth, bullet-shaped fuselage and a single vertical tail fin. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, each bearing a prominent white and blue U.S. Air Force roundel insignia. The cockpit is located near the front of the fuselage and appears to be a single-seat, enclosed with a dark canopy. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The airplane has no visible external engines, suggesting an internal jet engine configuration, and there are no visible landing gear components. The surface is glossy and metallic, with minimal surface features aside from insignia and panel lines.
description: A bright orange, streamlined military jet airplane with mid-mounted straight wings, a single vertical tail fin, and U.S. Air Force insignia. The cockpit is forward and enclosed, and the surface is glossy and metallic.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bullet-shaped fuselage, mid-mounted straight wing with insignia, and single vertical tail fin.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and the symmetrical wing layout.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 0, with visible insignia and tail number.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, cockpit placement, and the overall streamlined shape.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible dihedral or anhedral angle, no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Single internal jet engine, no external nacelles visible.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No landing gear visible; likely retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, single-seat, enclosed cockpit with a dark canopy.
